data.title: Monroe University is under investigation for a data breach affecting 320,973 records, as reported by Schubert Jonckheer & Kolbe LLP.
data.description: A law firm, Schubert Jonckheer & Kolbe LLP, is currently looking into a data breach that has affected approximately 320,973 individuals linked to Monroe University in New York. The breach has compromised sensitive personal information. All individuals associated with Monroe University are advised to be cautious about their privacy due to this incident.
Shanghai’s most active tin futures contract rose more than 7%.
PBOC raises the $CNY Central Parity Rate by 56 pips to 7.0064 per USD, the strongest level since May 18, 2023. $USDCNY $USDCNH

data.title: Senator Lummis predicts the market structure bill may face a delay due to debate on DeFi and stablecoin rewards in the CLARITY Act. Stakeholders clash over competing agendas.
data.description: The CLARITY Act is facing potential delays due to disagreements surrounding DeFi and stablecoin reward provisions. There is a contentious debate between banking and crypto stakeholders, with each group advocating for their own interests. This conflict poses a challenge for the bill, which is crucial for regulating the financial sector. The future of the bill remains uncertain as key decision-makers navigate the competing agendas. It is imperative to find common ground to move forward with this legislation.
Taiwan says it and the U.S. have held multiple talks and agreed on preferential tariffs for semiconductors under Section 232.
Taiwan government confirms tariff negotiators en route to U.S. for discussions.
Taiwan says both sides are expected to announce the consensus reached after consultations.

data.title: Cogeco Communications reported Q1 2026 results with $707.2m revenue, $353.8m EBITDA, and $93.1m profit. Positive changes in U.S. subscriber trends. Network upgrades include 2.5 Gigabit speeds in the U.S. New digital brand to launch in the U.S. Credit outlooks upgraded by S&P and Moody's. Dividend increased by 7.0% to $0.987 per share.
data.description: Cogeco Communications Inc. announced its financial results for the first quarter ended November 30, 2025. Revenue decreased by 4.3% to $707.2 million, with adjusted EBITDA dropping by 3.1% to $353.8 million. Profit for the period was $93.1 million. In the U.S., subscriber trends improved, leading to the best metrics in 15 quarters. Canadian customer growth continued, with network upgrades ongoing. Credit outlooks by S&P and Moody's improved, and fiscal 2026 financial guidelines were re-confirmed. Dividend per share increased by 7.0% to $0.987. Network expansion saw over 4,000 homes passed in Q1, with capital intensity at 22.2%. Acquisition of property, plant, and equipment increased by 2.6% to $157.2 million, while free cash flow decreased by 15.7% to $125.5 million. Operating activities cash flow decreased by 19.4% to $176.3 million. These changes were seen across revenue, adjusted EBITDA, profit, cash flow, and capital expenditures, impacting the financial performance of Cogeco Communications Inc.
